items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Holy Cross Abbey
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* the heartlands
*County:* County Tipperary
*Type:* abbey
*Period:* 12th century
*Coords:* 52.63916667, -7.86805556
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holy_Cross_Abbey
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Holy%20Cross%20Abbey/@52.641,-7.86,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/40/IMGHolyCross_4780w.jpg/330px-IMGHolyCross_4780w.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://holycrossabbey.ie/


*Description:*
Holy Cross Abbey (Mainistir na Croise Naofa) was a Cistercian monastery in Holycross near Thurles, County Tipperary, Ireland, situated on the River Suir. It takes its name from a relic of the True Cross or Holy Rood.

_Listing 1 of 79_

listing.desc: Holy Cross Abbey (Mainistir na Croise Naofa) was a Cistercian monastery in Holycross near Thurles, County Tipperary, Ireland, situated on the River Suir. It takes its name from a relic of the True Cross or Holy Rood.

*Name:* Cabra Castle
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* ulster and the north
*County:* County Cavan
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 18th century
*Coords:* 53.9138, -6.7745
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cabra_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Cabra%20Castle/@53.862,-6.872,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/places/ANXAkqFS22n1AfZBIVzHib--30ww5mLGjBV_eqPEnNDfr72K5xyZl3AaQhgplXfuPi93Nlaj2hun_RNfugNhQ9ZGNO6Bl4be5NXeyaw=s4800-w800
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://www.cabracastle.com/ , tag as hotel 

*Description:*
Cabra Castle is the name given to two castles, one now ruined, the other now used as a luxury hotel. Named for the nearby townland of Cabra, the sites of both structures are on the northside of Kingscourt in County Cavan, Ireland.

_Listing 2 of 79_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Wray Castle
*Country:* england
*Region:* northern england
*County:* Cumbria
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 19th century
*Coords:* 54.4006345, -2.9641913
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wray_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Wray%20Castle/@54.381,-2.953,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/24/Wray_Castle_2008.jpg/330px-Wray_Castle_2008.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* unknown
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Wray Castle is a Victorian neo-gothic building at Claife in Cumbria within the boundaries of the historic county of Lancashire. The house and grounds have belonged to the National Trust since 1929.
The Castle was open to the public for a dozen years prior to 2024. The Castle is now closed for refurbishment until 2027.

_Listing 6 of 79_

listing.name: Wray Castle
listing.desc:
Wray Castle is a Victorian neo-gothic building at Claife in Cumbria within the boundaries of the historic county of Lancashire. The house and grounds have belonged to the National Trust since 1929.
The Castle was open to the public for a dozen years prior to 2024. The Castle is now closed for refurbishment until 2027.

*Name:* Conishead Priory
*Country:* england
*Region:* northern england
*County:* Cumbria
*Type:* abbey
*Period:* 12th century / 19th century rebuild
*Coords:* 54.1732, -3.067
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conishead_Priory
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Conishead%20Priory/@54.1567,-3.1067,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b9/ConisheadPriory.JPG/330px-ConisheadPriory.JPG
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://manjushri.org/
Update google maps link - https://maps.app.goo.gl/sVPd9DNJMZF4kRAk8

*Description:*
Conishead Priory is a large Gothic Revival building on the Furness peninsula near Ulverston in Cumbria. The priory's name translates literally as "King's Hill Priory". Since 1976, the building has been occupied by a Buddhist community.

_Listing 7 of 79_
